title: E-passport%20Upgrade%20Department%20of%20Immigration%20and%20Citizenship%20Services%20(DOICS)%20Malawi%20Case%20Study
content:
CASE STUD Y
Design, Implementation, 
Integration, Supply, and 
Maintenance of a Secure 
e-Passport Personalization and 
Issuance System for the Department 
of Immigration and Citizenship 
Services, Republic of MalawiUPGRADE OF THE PASSPORT 
ISSUANCE SYSTEM AND 
INTRODUCTION OF 
ELECTRONIC PASSPORTS

The Department of Immigration and Citizenship Services, under 
Malawi's Ministry of Home Aﬀairs and Internal Security, manages 
border control, travel documents, permits, visas, and citizenship for 
eligible individuals. 
Established on January 1, 1964, after Malawi assumed immigration 
services from Rhodesia's Federal Government, the department has 
evolved from being part of the Malawi Police Services and Oﬃce of 
the President to its current role. Its mission is to ensure eﬀective 
migration management and timely issuance of relevant documents, 
supporting national security and socioeconomic development. The 
department remains a cornerstone of public service, enhancing 
governance and contributing to the country's growth.
The following are key values driving operations of the Department 
of Immigration and Citizenship Services:
• Good Customer Service 
• Transparency and Accountability
• Integrity and Honesty
• Responsiveness and resourcefulness 
• Mutual Trust
• Innovativeness 
• Flexibility
The Department of Immigration and Citizenship Services (DoICS) 
operated four passport application and issuing centers: Blantyre 
Headquarters, Lilongwe, Mzuzu, and Mangochi. Applications were 
centrally processed at the DoICS Headquarters server in Blantyre, 
with Machine-Readable Passports (MRPs) printed at all four centers 
based on the application location. 
To enhance service delivery, the Government of Malawi, through 
DoICS, implemented an electronic Passport (e-Passport) system 
aligned with International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) security 
standards. This initiative included upgrading hardware and network 
infrastructure and integrating e-Passports into the operational 
framework to provide secure and eﬃcient passport issuance.
Techno Brain has implemented an advanced and secure e-Passport 
Personalization and Issuance System, integrating state-of-the-art 
biometric enrollment and de-duplication of applications, seamless 
integration with the National Registration Bureau and banks, 
issuance management software, newly designed and 
ICAO-compliant chip-based e-Passport booklets and consumables, 
and a modern Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) setup.
Key Highlights of the project include:
• The Malawian e-Passport, featuring an entirely new 
conceptual security design, incorporates multiple security 
features that render counterfeiting impossible.• 
• 
• Business Situa tion

Solution:Quick Shee t
Department of Immigration and Citizenship 
Services (DOICS)
DoICS operated four passport centers, centrally 
processing in Blantyre, but faced poor service 
delivery.
Malawi implemented an ICAO-compliant 
e-Passport system, upgrading infrastructure for 
secure and eﬃcient passport issuance.
The Department improved passport processing, 
integrated systems, reduced fraud, and enabled 
real-time application tracking online.• The data page of the passport holder is coated with an 
ultra-thin transparent /f_ilm containing highly sophisticated and 
unique security aspects known in the industry.
• The data page of the passport holder integrates three levels of 
security: those veri/f_ied by visual inspection, those veri/f_ied by 
simple instruments, and those veri/f_ied by a specialized forensic 
lab. This integrated approach signi/f_icantly enhances passport 
security and enables both visual and automated detection of 
simulations and counterfeits.
• The bearer's photo, printed with special secure inks, exhibits a 
distinctive quality appearance and contains encoded data 
invisible to the naked eye, detectable only with the aid of 
special lenses and UV light.
• The various security elements are reinforced by robust 
back-end systems featuring sophisticated biometric 
authentication and Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) systems.
• Establishment of a modern data center to host centralized data 
of passport applications and historical data.
• Setting up a Forensic Lab at airports for the detection of 
fraudulent travel documents.
• Digitization of historic passport application /f_iles and supporting 
documents.

 
 Technologies

• Upgrade current Passport Issuance system to e-passport system.
• Design e-passport security features and produce the e-Passport 
Booklets
• Computerize passport registration in 10 Malawian embassies 
worldwide.
• Digitize the current passport registry.
• Supply of Server and Storage Hardware and set up and 
Con/f_iguration of a Primary Data Center and Disaster Recovery Site
• Data migration of existing records on to the new system
• Develop stock management system 
• Develop an Online Portal for Passport Application Status
• Set up and Con/f_iguration of 2 Forensic Labs for identifying 
fraudulent passports.
• Install Security cameras and Biometric Access Control to various 
e-Passport Issuance facilities.

integration with banks has eﬀectively 
eradicated /f_inancial fraud.
